title: The House amid the Reeds · I

layers.label: Orientation
layers.description: The whole passage in English, before the Japanese begins to enter.

layers.label: The place
layers.description: Three names set the tale in the old landscape of Mamma.

layers.label: The man
layers.description: Katsushirō enters through Akinari’s old narrative formula.

layers.label: Inheritance
layers.description: The continuity of the household appears.

layers.label: The fields
layers.description: Possession and material abundance enter the sentence.

layers.label: Prosperity
layers.description: The inherited household reaches its former fullness.

layers.label: Disposition
layers.description: Katsushirō’s inward nature begins to turn the story.

layers.label: Aversion
layers.description: His dislike of farming becomes the governing cause.

layers.label: Original
layers.description: The opening passage now stands in Akinari’s Japanese.
